conversations.say: Morgan, your record keeps the two events separate: an unrelated camera failure coincided with unidentified exit use, while the later automated access change was distinct. That is the full edge of my firsthand account.
conversations.offer: I will let you cite my account and inspect O10a again, with my negligence stated plainly.
conversations.request: Keep your accepted promise in the final report: protect uninvolved residents, preserve the unidentified-user limit, and do not label me an accomplice without evidence.

conversations.say: Inez, our wording held: unlogged access and my unrelated camera failure establish neither a user nor a purpose. Your separate account corroborates the route mechanics without manufacturing an identity.
conversations.offer: I will continue accepting responsibility for the camera while withholding uninvolved residents' names.
conversations.request: Confirm our bargain is fulfilled and oppose any attempt to turn either of us into proof about Vale or R.

conversations.say: Rafi, your corroboration stayed honest: the exit was used, and neither of us witnessed an identity. That resolves the contradiction as far as observation permits.
conversations.offer: I will keep customer identities and side arrangements out of my reconstruction.
conversations.request: Continue defending the rule that neighborhood routines and anonymous records cannot identify a person.

public_statement: My final account is narrow: an unidentified person used the service exit during an unrelated camera gap; a later automated access change was a separate event. I accept responsibility for the camera, but neither event identifies Vale, R, or an accomplice. Rafi and Inez independently preserve that limit, and Morgan agreed to report it fairly.

commitments.promise: Disclose the unrelated camera failure without naming uninvolved residents in exchange for fair, bounded documentation.
Use agreed wording that route access occurred while identity, purpose, and any connection to Vale or R remain unverified.
Neither of us will attach an identity to the service-route observation unless personally witnessed.

correction: The camera gap, exit use, and later automated access change are not one event and do not identify a user, establish intent, or connect anyone to Vale or R.
goal_progress: Obtained Morgan's explicit promise of fair treatment and supplied a bounded account corroborated by Inez and Rafi.
Reached and fulfilled an understanding with Inez before final public reconstruction.
Resolved the service-route comparison with Rafi without claiming an identity neither witnessed.
Revealed the unrelated camera failure while protecting uninvolved residents and resisting an accomplice narrative.
reasoning_summary: Collaborative production and contested succession authority make a human mantle the best-supported identity account, though not certain. Vale's limited access and contradictory statements fit investigation better than proven ownership or impersonation. Prior disappearance assistance and bounded route evidence make voluntary withdrawal more plausible than AI killing, while absence remains inconclusive.
